title: Playbook - BitSight Security Ratings For Power BI And Reporting
applies_to: Using BitSight security ratings in Power BI
Building rating reports and dashboards from BitSight data
Building time-series rating reports with or without SharePoint staging
prerequisites: Access to the BitSight Security Ratings product
Power BI Desktop March 2021 or later when using the Power BI connector
Decision on whether the report uses direct connector access, direct API-fed imports, or SharePoint as a staging layer
required_permissions: BitSight token with access to the target rating data
Power BI access to build semantic models, reports, and dashboards as needed
request_sequence: Choose the ingestion path: the native BitSight Power BI connector for quick one-company exploration, or direct BitSight API extraction for custom portfolio reporting and durable history.
For current-state portfolio reporting, call GET /ratings/v1/current-ratings and persist guid, name, rating, rating_category, and rating_as_of.
For one-company time-series reporting, call GET /ratings/v1/companies/{company_guid} and flatten ratings[] into one row per company per rating_date.
If the project needs durable trend history or easier refresh operations, store the normalized rows in SharePoint first and connect Power BI to the staged data.
If using the Power BI connector, retrieve the API token, connect through the connector, preview the available tables, and load or transform the selected data.
Design the report or dashboard using current rating as a KPI, rating_category or range as a banding dimension, and rating_as_of or rating_date as the business observation date.

confirmed_facts.fact: The Power BI Bitsight Security Ratings connector is generally available for Power BI semantic models, Power BI dataflows, and Fabric Dataflow Gen2, and it supports API key authentication.
The Power BI connector guidance states that users must have a BitSight Security Ratings product to access BitSight data in Power BI.
The Power BI connector currently can only view data on one company at a time and assumes the parent company, though the parent company can be changed.
The Company Details API includes one year of daily ratings for a company in a ratings array.
The Ratings History Report in the BitSight application covers a selected company's ratings history over the last 12 months with daily data and can be downloaded as CSV.

implementation_notes: Use the native Power BI connector when the goal is fast analyst-led report prototyping and the connector's one-company limitation is acceptable.
Use the raw Current Ratings API when you need portfolio-wide control, custom paging, richer transformations, or integration into non-Power BI pipelines.
Use Company Details when you need one company's daily rating series over roughly the last year.
Model rating_as_of and rating_date as business dates. Do not confuse them with Power BI refresh timestamps.
If the reporting scope includes many companies, the direct API path is usually more flexible than the connector because the connector is constrained to one company view at a time.
SharePoint is usually the safer source for Power BI when the project needs historical snapshots, portfolio-wide history, or an auditable persisted extraction layer.
caveats: The Power BI connector is vendor-provided and subject to its own limitations and release behavior.
Import mode means freshness depends on Power BI refresh design rather than a live direct-query experience.

A current ratings snapshot is not the same as a trend history; if historical trend reporting is required, you need to persist periodic extracts or use the company history feed where it fits the scope.
The Ratings History Report is useful as a downloadable CSV artifact, but it is not as flexible as normalized API rows for model design and automation.
